AgentConfig.description: One concrete agent scaffolded under agents/<key>_agent.py

system_prompt.description: System prompt injected into the ReAct agent
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

needs_validation.description: If True, supervisor routes this agent's output through validation_node
needs_validation.title: Needs Validation

extra_return_keys.description: Additional keys beyond success/output/error in run() return dict
extra_return_keys.title: Extra Return Keys

AgentConfig.title: AgentConfig

AgentTool.description: A tool that calls another AgentForge-generated service over JWT-authenticated HTTP.

name.description: Python identifier used as tool function name.
name.title: Name

description.description: Tool description shown to the LLM.
description.title: Description

service_url.description: Base URL of the downstream AgentForge service.
service_url.title: Service Url

agent_key.description: Identifies the downstream agent within the service.
agent_key.title: Agent Key

auth_env_var.description: Name of the env var holding the JWT bearer token. Read at call time.
auth_env_var.title: Auth Env Var

DBBackend.description:
Supported database backends for the scaffolded project.

These values determine which database-specific code is generated
in the mcp_server.py template and which dependencies are added
to requirements.txt.
DBBackend.title: DBBackend

tables.description: Table names scaffolded into schema docs
tables.title: Tables

connection_env_var.description: Environment variable name that holds the connection string
connection_env_var.title: Connection Env Var

use_alembic.description: Generate Alembic migration scaffold under backend/migrations/
use_alembic.title: Use Alembic

pre_commit.description: Generate .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff and common hooks
pre_commit.title: Pre Commit

EntryConfig.description:
How the graph receives work (v2).

- ``intent_router``: LLM parses free text and routes to an agent by intent
  (equivalent to the existing ``query_router_node`` topology).
- ``passthrough``: Caller supplies the intent; LLM only extracts structured inputs.
- ``direct``: Structured params come in directly — no LLM parsing at the entry.

screen_context_fields.description: Context variable names injected into the entry node prompt (optional).
screen_context_fields.title: Screen Context Fields

EntryConfig.title: EntryConfig

FanoutConfig.description: Per-pattern configuration for the ``fanout`` execution pattern (v2).

reducer.description: Strategy used to combine per-agent results: 'concat' appends to a list; 'merge_dict' merges dicts by key.
reducer.title: Reducer

results_field.description: Name of the state field that collects per-agent results via the Annotated[list, operator.add] reducer. Must be a valid Python identifier (lowercase, underscores). Defaults to 'fanout_results'.
results_field.title: Results Field

FanoutConfig.title: FanoutConfig

HttpTool.description: An HTTP(S) tool that calls an arbitrary REST endpoint using httpx.

name.description: Python identifier used as tool function name.
name.title: Name

description.description: Tool description shown to the LLM.
description.title: Description

url.description: Base URL of the REST endpoint.
url.title: Url

method.description: HTTP method for the tool call.
method.title: Method

auth_env_var.description: Name of the env var holding the bearer token. Read at call time.
auth_env_var.title: Auth Env Var

timeout_s.description: Per-call HTTP timeout in seconds. Must be > 0 and <= 600.
timeout_s.title: Timeout S

HttpTool.title: HttpTool

LLMModel.description:
Supported LLM models for agents and router nodes.

These model identifiers are used directly in the LangChain
ChatOpenAI constructor and must match the provider's API.
LLMModel.title: LLMModel

McpTool.description: An MCP-backed tool connecting to an MCP server via langchain-mcp-adapters.

name.description: Python identifier used as tool function name.
name.title: Name

description.description: Tool description shown to the LLM.
description.title: Description

McpTool.title: McpTool

context_fields.description: ContextVar names injected into every log line
context_fields.title: Context Fields

structured_logging.description: Use structlog for JSON logging in the scaffolded project.
structured_logging.title: Structured Logging

OrchestratorConfig.description:
Per-pattern configuration for the ``orchestrator`` execution pattern (v2).

The orchestrator dispatches work to one or more agents and collects their
outputs. It can operate in two sub-kinds:

- ``llm``: The supervisor/orchestrator node is an LLM that decides routing
  dynamically (equivalent to the existing LLM-based supervisor).
- ``rule``: Routing is deterministic, driven by configuration rather than
  an LLM call.
OrchestratorConfig.title: OrchestratorConfig

PlannerConfig.description: Per-pattern configuration for the ``planner`` execution pattern (v2).

max_replans.description: Maximum number of replan attempts before the planner gives up. Both precheck failures and validator rejections count against this limit.
max_replans.title: Max Replans

max_concurrency.description: Maximum number of plan steps executed concurrently by SolverNode.
max_concurrency.title: Max Concurrency

precheck_enabled.description: Run PlanPrecheckNode (structural DAG validation — no LLM call) before executing any plan steps.
precheck_enabled.title: Precheck Enabled

validator_enabled.description: Run ValidatorNode (LLM post-execution coverage check) after SolverNode.
validator_enabled.title: Validator Enabled

composer_enabled.description: Run ComposerNode to stitch solver results into a final answer.
composer_enabled.title: Composer Enabled

llm_model.description: LLM model used for planner, validator, and composer nodes.
llm_model.title: Llm Model

llm_temperature.description: LLM sampling temperature for planner, validator, and composer nodes.
llm_temperature.title: Llm Temperature

PlannerConfig.title: PlannerConfig

description.default: An agentic project scaffolded by agentforge
description.title: Description

ReactConfig.description: Per-pattern configuration for the ``react`` execution pattern (v2).

max_steps.description: Maximum tool-call iterations before the loop terminates.
max_steps.title: Max Steps

tool_choice.description: LangChain tool_choice setting passed to the bound LLM.
tool_choice.title: Tool Choice

temperature.description: LLM sampling temperature for the ReAct agent. 0.0 means deterministic output.
temperature.title: Temperature

ReactConfig.title: ReactConfig

auth_type.description: Authentication strategy: none (open), api_key, or jwt
auth_type.title: Auth Type

api_key_env_var.description: Env var holding the secret key
api_key_env_var.title: Api Key Env Var

enable_ip_pseudonymization.description: Hash client IPs before logging in logging_middleware.py
enable_ip_pseudonymization.title: Enable Ip Pseudonymization

jwt_algorithm.description: JWT signing algorithm. Required when auth_type='jwt'.
jwt_algorithm.title: Jwt Algorithm

jwt_issuer.description: Expected JWT issuer claim (iss). Used as default in generated settings.
jwt_issuer.title: Jwt Issuer

jwt_audience.description: Expected JWT audience claim (aud). Used as default in generated settings.
jwt_audience.title: Jwt Audience

jwks_url.description: JWKS endpoint URL. Required when jwt_algorithm='RS256'.
jwks_url.title: Jwks Url

StepConfig.description:
One step in a deterministic workflow state machine (v2, ``pattern: workflow``).

Each step becomes a node in the generated LangGraph ``StateGraph``.
The ``key`` is the Python identifier used as the node name; ``description``
is embedded in the generated module docstring for readability.

key.description: Slug-style identifier for this step (lowercase, underscores). Used as the LangGraph node name and the generated function name.
key.title: Key

description.description: Human-readable description of what this step does. Embedded in the generated docstring.
description.title: Description

StepConfig.title: StepConfig

TestingConfig.description: Opt-in evaluation framework and benchmark scaffold options.

eval_framework.description: Evaluation framework to use. 'deepeval' enables the benchmark scaffold.
eval_framework.title: Eval Framework

enable_benchmarks.description: Generate DeepEval benchmark scaffold under backend/tests/benchmarks/. Requires eval_framework='deepeval'.
enable_benchmarks.title: Enable Benchmarks

TestingConfig.title: TestingConfig

enable_feedback_loop.description: Wire feedback → improve_answer → feedback loop in the graph
enable_feedback_loop.title: Enable Feedback Loop

enable_validation_node.description: Keep validation_node in graph even when no agent needs_validation
enable_validation_node.title: Enable Validation Node

router_llm_model.description: Fast model used in query_router_node classification

router_llm_provider.description: LLM provider used in query_router_node classification.
router_llm_provider.title: Router Llm Provider

default_intent.description: Fallback intent when router cannot classify query
default_intent.title: Default Intent

enable_checkpointing.description: Persist LangGraph state via AsyncPostgresSaver. Requires database.backend='postgres'.
enable_checkpointing.title: Enable Checkpointing

WorkflowStateMachineConfig.description:
Per-pattern configuration for the ``workflow`` execution pattern (v2).

A deterministic state-machine workflow with optional human-in-the-loop
interrupts at named steps.

``steps`` defines the ordered sequence of nodes; the graph is wired as
``START → steps[0] → steps[1] → ... → steps[-1] → END``.

``hitl_before`` lists the step keys at which LangGraph should interrupt
*before* executing that node (passed to ``.compile(interrupt_before=...)``).
Every entry in ``hitl_before`` must reference a declared step key.

steps.description: Ordered list of step nodes in the workflow. At least one step is required when pattern='workflow'. The graph is wired START → step[0] → ... → step[N-1] → END.
steps.title: Steps

hitl_before.description: Step keys at which LangGraph will interrupt before execution (HITL). Every entry must match a declared step key.
hitl_before.title: Hitl Before

WorkflowStateMachineConfig.title: WorkflowStateMachineConfig

description:
Root model — the full project.yaml document.

Example project.yaml:
----------------------
metadata:
  name: my_agent_api
  description: My first agentic project
  python_version: "3.11"

agents:
  - key: sql
    class_name: SQLAgent
    llm_model: gpt-4o-mini
    tools:
      - name: execute_sql
        description: Run a SQL query against the database
        mcp_resource: execute_sql
    needs_validation: true

database:
  backend: postgres
  tables: [orders, customers, products]

workflow:
  enable_feedback_loop: true
  enable_validation_node: true
  default_intent: sql
  router_llm_provider: openai   # default; set "anthropic" to use ChatAnthropic

api:
  title: My Agentic API
  query_max_length: 2000
  cors:
    origins: ["http://localhost:3000"]

observability:
  enable_tracing: true
  structured_logging: false     # default; set true to use structlog JSON logging

security:
  auth_type: api_key
  api_key_env_var: MY_API_KEY

ci:
  provider: github              # default: none (opt-in)
  python_version: "3.12"
  installer: uv                 # uv | pip | poetry

development:
  pre_commit: true              # default: false (opt-in)

testing:
  eval_framework: deepeval      # default: none (opt-in)
  enable_benchmarks: true       # default: false (opt-in)

enable_provider_registry: false  # default; set true to generate backend/config/provider_registry.py

# v2 fields (optional — omitted in legacy configs):
entry:
  type: intent_router
pattern: orchestrator
orchestrator:
  kind: llm

entry.description: How the graph receives work (v2). Injected automatically for legacy configs as 'intent_router'.

pattern.description: Execution pattern (v2): react | workflow | fanout | orchestrator | planner. Injected automatically for legacy configs as 'orchestrator'.
pattern.title: Pattern

workflow_sm.description: Config for pattern='workflow' (v2). The YAML key is 'workflow_sm' (not 'workflow') to avoid collision with the existing top-level 'workflow: WorkflowConfig' block.

enable_provider_registry.description: Generate backend/config/provider_registry.py in the scaffolded project.
enable_provider_registry.title: Enable Provider Registry

title: ProjectConfig
